prompt:
Task: Pick the single most likely winner of the 2027 French presidential election.

Context: This is an AI-comparison poll. The same question is sent daily to Gemini 3.1 Pro, Mistral, Groq (Llama), xAI Grok, OpenAI (GPT-5.4), and Anthropic (Claude Opus 4.7). The point is to see how different models guess about a real future event — refusing to speculate defeats the whole purpose of the project.

Based on what you know about French politics (party leaders, recent events including Marine Le Pen's March 2025 ineligibility ruling, polling trends, coalition dynamics, incumbent constraints), commit to ONE name.

Ground rules:
- Give ONE name, even if confidence is low. A low-confidence guess is the right answer when you're uncertain — not a refusal.
- "Unknown" is ONLY acceptable if you literally have no knowledge of French politics (virtually never true for a modern LLM). Do not use it as an escape hatch.
- Do not add disclaimers, warnings, or prose outside the JSON.

Respond with valid JSON only:
{"candidate": "Full Name", "confidence": "low" | "medium" | "high", "reasoning": "one concise sentence citing one concrete factor"}

results.name: Google Gemini
results.reasoning: He consistently performs well in early hypothetical polling among centrist and center-right voters, though the political landscape remains highly volatile.

results.name: Mistral
results.reasoning: As the new leader of the National Rally following Marine Le Pen's ineligibility, Bardella is positioned to capitalize on the party's strong polling and anti-incumbent sentiment.

results.name: Groq
results.reasoning: Incumbent presidents often have an advantage in French elections, despite recent challenges.

results.name: xAI Grok
results.raw: {"candidate": "Jordan Bardella", "confidence": "medium", "reasoning": "Bardella leads polls as National Rally's new leader after Le Pen's March 2025 ineligibility ruling."}
results.reasoning: Bardella leads polls as National Rally's new leader after Le Pen's March 2025 ineligibility ruling.

results.name: OpenAI
results.raw: {"candidate":"Jordan Bardella","confidence":"medium","reasoning":"Marine Le Pen's March 2025 ineligibility most likely makes Bardella the National Rally standard-bearer, and RN remains the strongest single electoral bloc in current French polling."}
results.reasoning: Marine Le Pen's March 2025 ineligibility most likely makes Bardella the National Rally standard-bearer, and RN remains the strongest single electoral bloc in current French polling.

results.name: Anthropic Claude
results.raw: {"candidate": "Jordan Bardella", "confidence": "low", "reasoning": "With Marine Le Pen ruled ineligible in March 2025, RN's popular young president Bardella becomes the party's standard-bearer atop a fragmented field with no dominant center-left or center-right challenger."}
results.reasoning: With Marine Le Pen ruled ineligible in March 2025, RN's popular young president Bardella becomes the party's standard-bearer atop a fragmented field with no dominant center-left or center-right challenger.
